sourceText: Poetry is a bride whose face is unveiled only to the one who has courted her with long vigils.
True mastery of language and art requires immense dedication and effort.
The world is a stage, and its people are but players in a tale told by a clever tongue.
Reflects the themes of his work, suggesting that life can be seen as a series of performances and stories, where wit and eloquence often determine the outcome.
A lifetime of exile, imprisonment, and persecution by the secularist regime.
Transformed suffering into spiritual work, writing the Risale-i Nur and inspiring a vast faith movement.
All things are beautiful, either in themselves or in their results.
This means finding divine beauty and purpose even in things that seem ugly or painful. Every event holds a positive outcome or lesson.
The universe is a great book, and we are its readers.
This encourages the study of creation (science) as a way to understand the Creator. Observing nature is like reading God's artistry and wisdom.
He who sees the good in a thing, sees the good in everything.
A positive perspective has the power to transform any experience. Faith provides a lens through which we can see God's goodness in all things.
Born on the Kipchak Steppe, Baybars was captured as a boy and sold into the brutal world of slavery, his fate seemingly sealed by forces beyond his control.

Through extraordinary military skill and ruthless ambition, he rose from a slave to a Mamluk commander, ultimately seizing the sultanate and becoming the bulwark that saved Egypt and Syria from both the Crusaders and the Mongols.
